rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc.'s Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories is DENIED. If responding party fails to respond, all that need be shown in the moving papers is that a set of interrogatories was properly served on the opposing party, that the time to respond has expired, and that no response of any kind has been served. See Leach v. Sup. Ct. (Markum) (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 905-906. No separate statement is required, and the moving party is not required to show a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the matter informally with opposing counsel. CCP Section 2030.290; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 411. Here, Stenkamp and Vanguard did not show in the moving papers that their special interrogatories were properly served. The basic element of service is set out in CCP Section 2030.080(a): "The party propounding interrogatories shall serve a copy of them on the party to whom the interrogatories are directed." No Proof of Service was presented in the moving papers to evidence service on Lawrence Porchia or Christiane Hipps, Porchia's attorney of record on January 19, 2021. The Proof of Service attached to Stenkamp and Vanguard's special interrogatories stated that electronic service was made on January 19, 2021, to Keystone Law Group, Inc. Keystone Law Group, Inc. ceased representing Porchia in June of 2019. Stenkamp and Vanguard state in their moving papers that Porchia was served again on March 9, 2021; but, no Proof of Service was presented in the moving papers to support the claim. On the date for oppositions to this motion, Stenkamp and Vanguard's counsel submitted a Supplemental Declaration attaching a proof of service indicating service of the discovery on Hipps and Porchia on March 9, 2021. They served the Supplemental Declaration on Porchia, who is now self-represented, from San Diego by "U.S. Mail," not a method reasonably calculated to provide Porchia timely notice of the evidence they were relying on to carry their initial burden. Furthermore, the Supplemental Declaration did not include the date or location of this hearing as required by CRC 3.110(b)(1); such an error is especially problematic when at the time of service the serving party has at least five motions set for at least three different hearing/dates. Stenkamp and Vanguard's evidentiary submission is untimely. A moving party cannot satisfy its initial burden on a motion by an evidentiary submission served on the opposing party on the day the opposition is due, and served in a manner not calculated for prompt receipt. Stenkamp and Vanguard's untimely submission is not excused. The record does not support a finding excusing the untimely evidentiary submission.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Deem Admissions Admitted; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc.'s Motion to Deem Admissions Admitted is DENIED. "The party requesting admissions shall serve a copy of them on the party to whom they are directed and on all other parties who have appeared in the action." CCP Section 2033.070. The responding party has 30 days after service of the requests for admission in which to respond. CCP Section 2033.250. If the party to whom requests for admission are directed fails to serve a timely reply, then the propounding party can move to have the requests deemed admitted. Here, Stenkamp and Vanguard did not establish in the moving papers that their requests for admissions were properly served. No Proof of Service has been presented in the moving papers to evidence service on Lawrence Porchia or Christiane Hipps, Porchia's attorney of record on January 19, 2021. The Proof of Service attached to Stenkamp and Vanguard's requests for admissions stated that electronic service was made on January 19, 2021, to Keystone Law Group, Inc. Keystone Law Group, Inc. ceased representing Porchia in June of 2019. Stenkamp and Vanguard state in their moving papers that Porchia was served again on March 9, 2021; but, no such Proof of Service was presented with the moving papers to support the claim. On the date for oppositions to this motion, Stenkamp and Vanguard's counsel submitted a Supplemental Declaration attaching a proof of service indicating service of the discovery on Hipps and Porchia on March 9, 2021. They served the Supplemental Declaration on Porchia, who is now self-represented, from San Diego by "U.S. Mail," not a method reasonably calculated to provide Porchia timely notice of the evidence they were relying on to carry their initial burden. Furthermore, the Supplemental Declaration did not include the date or location of this hearing as required by CRC 3.110(b)(1); such an error is especially problematic when at the time of service the serving party has at least five motions set for at least three different hearing/dates. Stenkamp and Vanguard's evidentiary submission is untimely. A moving party cannot satisfy its initial burden on a motion by an evidentiary submission served on the opposing party on the day the opposition is a due, and served in a manner not calculated for prompt receipt. Stenkamp and Vanguard's untimely submission is not excused. The record does not support a finding excusing the untimely evidentiary submission. Stenkamp and Vanguard cite Sexton v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403 at page 1410 for the proposition that "Porchia's failure to submit a written opposition to a pending motion precludes him from presenting oral argument at the hearing on the motion." This is not a holding in the case. The reference is to a portion of Los Angeles County Superior Court Rule 9.15. In pertinent part rule 9.15 provides: "A party who has not timely filed written opposition to a motion or demurrer may not be afforded an opportunity to offer oral argument at the hearing. The failure to file opposition creates an inference that the motion or demurrer is meritorious. In such cases, the court may hear argument limited to a request for a continuance of the hearing in order to afford an opportunity for written opposition." First, the Los Angeles Superior Court local rules have no application in this court and should not be cited to this court for any proposition. Second, even if the rule had some persuasive value-and it does not-it's permissive ("may") not mandatory ("precludes"). Counsel is advised to take care with the authorities they cite.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Stenkamp and Vanguard cite Sexton v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403 at page 1410 for the proposition that "Porchia's failure to submit a written opposition to a pending motion precludes him from presenting oral argument at the hearing on the motion." This is not a holding in the case. The reference is to a portion of Los Angeles County Superior Court Rule 9.15. In pertinent part rule 9.15 provides: "A party who has not timely filed written opposition to a motion or demurrer may not be afforded an opportunity to offer oral argument at the hearing. The failure to file opposition creates an inference that the motion or demurrer is meritorious. In such cases, the court may hear argument limited to a request for a continuance of the hearing in order to afford an opportunity for written opposition." First, the Los Angeles Superior Court local rules have no application in this court and should not be cited to this court for any proposition. Second, even if the rule had some persuasive value-and it does not-it's permissive ("may") not mandatory ("precludes"). Counsel is advised to take care with the authorities they cite. Stenkamp and Vanguard's request for sanctions is DENIED. Stenkamp and Vanguard have not successfully made a motion to compel under CCP Section 2030.290(c). Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a petition for approval of a report of status of administration has been filed. The statutory filing deadline was 08/05/2021, as letters were issued to the administrator on 08/04/2020. A courtesy reminder of this continued status hearing was e-mailed to counsel on 11/16/2021. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
